The **2012 NBA Development League draft** was the 12th draft of the [National Basketball Association Development League](/wiki/NBA_G_League "NBA G League") (NBDL). The draft was held on November 2, 2012, before the [2012–13 season](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_NBA_Development_League_season "2012–13 NBA Development League season"). In this [draft](/wiki/Draft_(sports) "Draft (sports)"), all 16 of the league's teams took turns selecting eligible players.

[JaJuan Johnson](/wiki/JaJuan_Johnson "JaJuan Johnson") was the [first overall draft pick](/wiki/List_of_first_overall_NBA_Development_League_draft_picks "List of first overall NBA Development League draft picks").[[1]](#cite_note-1) In [2011–12](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_NBA_season "2011–12 NBA season") he had played for the [Boston Celtics](/wiki/Boston_Celtics "Boston Celtics") after being selected in the [2011 NBA draft](/wiki/2011_NBA_draft "2011 NBA draft").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He became the second "Johnson" taken number one overall in the NBDL's after [Ken Johnson](/wiki/Ken_Johnson_(basketball,_born_1978) "Ken Johnson (basketball, born 1978)") was chosen in [2003](/wiki/2003_NBDL_Draft "2003 NBDL Draft"). The first non-American selected was [Christian Eyenga](/wiki/Christian_Eyenga "Christian Eyenga"), who was taken eighth by the [Texas Legends](/wiki/Texas_Legends "Texas Legends"). Ten players had also been selected previously in an [NBA draft](/wiki/NBA_draft "NBA draft") – JaJuan Johnson,[[3]](#cite_note-DRAFT11-3) [Andrew Goudelock](/wiki/Andrew_Goudelock "Andrew Goudelock"),[[3]](#cite_note-DRAFT11-3) [Justin Harper](/wiki/Justin_Harper_(basketball) "Justin Harper (basketball)"),[[3]](#cite_note-DRAFT11-3) [Shelvin Mack](/wiki/Shelvin_Mack "Shelvin Mack"),[[3]](#cite_note-DRAFT11-3) [Shan Foster](/wiki/Shan_Foster "Shan Foster"),[[4]](#cite_note-DRAFT08-4) Christian Eyenga,[[5]](#cite_note-DRAFT09-5) [Jack McClinton](/wiki/Jack_McClinton "Jack McClinton"),[[5]](#cite_note-DRAFT09-5) [Travis Leslie](/wiki/Travis_Leslie "Travis Leslie"),[[3]](#cite_note-DRAFT11-3) [DeVon Hardin](/wiki/DeVon_Hardin "DeVon Hardin"),[[4]](#cite_note-DRAFT08-4) and [Sean Singletary](/wiki/Sean_Singletary "Sean Singletary").[[4]](#cite_note-DRAFT08-4)

Although some of the players chosen in the 2012 NBA Development League Draft had played semi-professional and/or professional basketball after college graduation, only the United States colleges they attended are listed.
